stories.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
stories.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

stories.title: U.S. seeks NATO help with Strait of Hormuz. And, federal judge blocks vaccine changes
stories.description: As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.

stories.title: A Trump official quits over the Iran war, as Israel says it killed 2 Iranian commanders
stories.description: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani. Iran has yet to confirm but it would be the highest-profile killings since the targeting of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah
stories.description: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.

stories.title: Historian shares how Russia benefits from the US being at war with Iran
stories.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.

stories.title: How Trump's Treasury is shifting sanctions to punish his critics and reward friends
stories.description: Spain's Prime Minister called U.S. strikes against Iran "unjustified." When other foreigners in power have used similar language against the U.S. or Israel, they were sanctioned by the Treasury.

stories.title: How the energy crisis fueled by the war in Iran is affecting Asia
stories.description: Asia is feeling the energy shock of the war on Iran. NPR's A Martinez talks to Sam Reynolds of the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis.

stories.title: Morning news brief
stories.description: Israel's ground moves in Lebanon raise pressure on Western countries, Europeans leaders reject Trump's demand to help with the Strait of Hormuz, blackout leaves millions without power in Cuba.

stories.title: Millions of migrants in the Middle East caught in the middle of the war in Iran
stories.description: As the war in Iran continues, millions of migrant workers in the Gulf are caught up in the ongoing conflict, adding to their already vulnerable existence.

stories.title: Trump says Europeans should help secure the Strait of Hormuz. So far, they've refused
stories.description: European leaders are rejecting President Trump's demand they send military assets to the Middle East to help open the Strait of Hormuz, which is being obstructed by Iran.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
stories.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Larijani's death would be a 'significant blow' but may not deter Iran
stories.description: Israel said Tuesday it had killed Iran's powerful national security chief, Ali Larijani, in what would be a huge blow to the Islamic republic, but probably not one that would deter Tehran from continuing the war against Israel and the US, says France 2''s correspondent in the Ira...

stories.title: Trump seeks to delay China visit as Beijing rebuffs Hormuz demands
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Monday said he is seeking to delay a highly anticipated trip to China in early April by about a month because of the Iran war. It comes after Trump called on numerous nations, including China, to help ships safely transit the Strait of Hormuz, a reque...

stories.title: Israel sends more troops to southern Lebanon as residents flee
stories.description: The Israeli military said Tuesday that additional troops have been deployed in southern Lebanon, as part of efforts to create an “additional layer of security for residents of northern Israel against Hezbollah's threat". It comes as shelters in the country fill up with more than ...

stories.title: Larijani's death would mean 'Iran's leadership very much infiltrated by Israeli intelligence'
stories.description: Israel said Tuesday it had killed Iran's powerful national security chief, Ali Larijani, in what would be a huge blow to the Islamic republic more than two weeks into the war engulfing the Middle East. It would also suggest Iran's senior leadership has been infiltrated by Israeli...

stories.title: Iran's Larijani - A pillar of power working from the shadows
stories.description: Iran’s security chief Ali Larijani, whom Israel claimed Tuesday to have killed in a strike, was a Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's right hand man and, following the supreme leader’s death early in the war, was believed to be effectively running the country. France 24’s Delano D’Souza tak...

stories.title: Decades of deployment: America's extensive military presence in the Middle East
stories.description: As yet another war rages in the Middle East, we take a closer look at the United States' military presence across the region. It's a presence that stretches back decades, but took on heightened visibility during the Iran-Iraq war of the 1980s, when both those nations began target...

stories.title: Lebanon: In the Bekaa Valley, a Christian village shelters displaced Shiite families
stories.description: In eastern Lebanon, the Baalbek region – a stronghold of Hezbollah – has repeatedly been targeted by Israeli strikes since March 2. Fleeing the bombardment, residents from the predominantly Shiite area have sought refuge in the nearby Christian village of Deir al-Ahmar. FRANCE 24...

stories.title: Guy Poran urges international community to demand Israeli govt end settler violence in West Bank
stories.description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Guy Poran, former Israeli Air Force Pilot, human rights activist and volunteer for Israeli peace activist group Mistaclim. According to Poran, the situation in the West Bank has entered a dangerous phase, following wars in Gaza, Lebanon and Iran. ...

stories.title: 'Coalition of the unwilling': Allies refuse to clean up Trump's Strait of Hormuz mess
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, March 17: Donald Trump has called on US allies to help the US secure the Strait of Hormuz as the energy crisis intensifies. But after those allies refused to help, Trump promptly threatened NATO. In the midst of the EU's energy crisis, Belgium's prime mini...

stories.title: Gulf states seek to bypass Strait of Hormuz for oil exports via pipelines
stories.description: Iran sends drones to attack the UAE port of Fujairah in the Gulf of Oman, raising the stakes as Gulf states send their blocked crude through pipelines away from the contested Strait of Hormuz. We take a closer look. Also in this edition: central banks face a dilemma as high oil p...

stories.title: Trump's call for help with Hormuz largely falls on deaf ears
stories.description: Donald Trump is finding himself increasingly isolated on the world stage with allies, including NATO countries, are refusing to come to his aid to help to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The EU meanwhile says dipolomatic ways have to found in order to keep the critical waterway open...

stories.title: 'Ready to fight' but lacking means, Iraqi Kurds targeted by Iran
stories.description: Iranian Kurdish opposition groups, united in a coalition since last February, issued a statement this weekend calling on all "oppressed minorities in Iran” to unite in support of a democratic transition. But in Iraqi Kurdistan, these Kurdish parties, in exile for decades, continu...

stories.title: Who is Gholamreza Soleimani, the commander of Iran’s Basij forces?
stories.description: Israel claims to have killed Soleimani. Here is what we know about the veteran commander.

stories.title: US National Counterterrorism Center director Joe Kent resigns over Iran war
stories.description: Kent, who advised Trump and national intelligence director on 'terror' threats, says Iran 'posed no imminent threat'.

stories.title: US envoys meet Hamas in Cairo to salvage fragile Gaza truce
stories.description: Rare US-Hamas talks come as the war on Iran strains the battered Gaza 'ceasefire' and post-war plans.

stories.title: Macron prepares France for ‘an age of nuclear weapons’ as Iran war rages
stories.description: The French president described his plans for expansion as forward deterrence. What's behind his move?

stories.title: The US-Israel war on Iran is shaped by religion as much as strategy
stories.description: The conflict has mutated into a zero-sum collision of competing messianic frameworks.

stories.title: Iran blames US, Israel for Hormuz tensions as crisis risks energy supplies
stories.description: Iran's Araghchi calls for global condemnation of ‘military aggression’ that disrupted transit through Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: UN warns of record hunger, 45 million more at risk, if Iran war continues
stories.description: The Middle East conflict risks adding a staggering 45 million to acute hunger, warns the UN's World Food Programme.

stories.title: Are the US and Israel waging war on Iran’s cultural heritage?
stories.description: Iran says at least 56 of its heritage sites damaged or destroyed in war.

stories.title: German Chancellor says Iran war has no military solution
stories.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said the Iran conflict cannot be resolved militarily and must be resolved politically.

stories.title: Could the Iran war trigger a global recession?
stories.description: Energy prices are surging as the Iran war disrupts supply, raising risks for the US, China and Europe.

stories.title: Israel says Iran’s security chief Larijani has been killed
stories.description: Israel’s Defence Minister Israel Katz says Ali Larijani, Iran’s security chief, has been killed in an Israeli strike.

stories.title: Israel says it’s killed Iran’s security chief Larijani and Basij commander
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Katz has claimed that Iran’s top security official has been killed; Iran is yet to comment.

stories.title: Gulf economies suffer brunt of Iran war as recession risk looms
stories.description: From energy exports to tourism, the economies of Gulf nations are at risk from a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

stories.title: Kenya may face fertilizer shortage due to conflict in the Middle East — radio station
stories.description: About 26% of all imported fertilizers are coming to Kenya via the Strait of Hormuz, the radio station said

stories.title: Rubio orders US diplomats to influence other nations to take action against Iran — TV
stories.description: The American diplomatic institutions are also instructed to "swiftly" urge the local governments to declare the IRGC and Hezbollah terrorist organizations

stories.title: Trump allies fear loss of control over situation around Iran
stories.description: According to the sources, some in the president’s circle believe a ground operation against Iran may be the only way to secure victory

stories.title: Tankers start crossing Hormuz, White House says
stories.description: It's a sign of how little Iran has left, Director of the US National Economic Council Kevin Hassett said

stories.title: FIFA hopes to see Iranian national team playing at 2026 World Cup
stories.description: Iran was scheduled to play all of its three group stage matches in the United States

stories.title: EU should return Russian oil to Europe — Szijjarto
stories.description: The situation around Iran caused the threat of "the historically most serious crisis in the sphere of oil supplies," the Hungarian top diplomat said

stories.title: Eurasian Development Bank projects oil prices in $75-85 per barrel range in 2026
stories.description: The EDB analysts added that "around 25% of global oil supplies and about 20% of liquefied gas supplies" pass through the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: US intelligence warned of risk of Iran striking Arab countries — Reuters
stories.description: The White House apparently ignored this information, the agency believes

stories.title: White House launched operation in Iran without clear strategy — NYT
stories.description: The first weeks of the operation against Iran "do not inspire confidence" and instead indicate that the behind-the-scenes planning in the White House may have been as reckless as its public behavior, the newspaper writes

stories.title: Iran in talks with FIFA on relocating team's 2026 World Cup matches from US to Mexico
stories.description: Iran was scheduled to play all of its three group stage matches with Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand in the United States
